structural_claim: Topological constraints fundamentally determine the allowed configurations and evolution of physical systems, with topology being conserved except at singular surfaces where the governing equations break down

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Topological soliton solutions arise necessarily from the topology of the vacuum manifold in spontaneously broken gauge theories
Topologically distinct vacuum configurations are connected by finite-action paths that determine non-perturbative physics
Magnetic field line topology is conserved in ideal plasmas, with the field frozen into the fluid motion
Topological linkage of magnetic field lines is conserved in ideal MHD, constraining plasma evolution to preserve helicity
Topology change requires breaking ideal constraints at singular current sheets where the governing equations fail
